app.name: Lexoni.ai
app.tagline: The practice operating system for GCC law firms

time.allCaughtUp: All caught up - {count} entries confirmed. Nothing leaking today.

desk.subtitle: Your desk - what needs you today, your book, and your numbers. The firm runs around you, not the case.

book.title: My book - by process & stage
book.subtitle: Open matters, where each one is in its process

matters.listSubtitle: Every matter runs on a process. Open one to work the ordered document set.

workspace.docSetSubtitle: Drag a template from the right onto any slot, or use the AI prompt.
workspace.promptPlaceholderText: Describe the change… e.g. 'mutual confidentiality, 30-day cure period'
workspace.promptPlaceholderVoice: Tap record, then dictate… (e.g. 'add a 3-year non-compete limited to Dubai')
workspace.suggestionNote: AI drafts into a suggestion state. A lawyer with review rights accepts - it never auto-finalises or crosses the wall.
workspace.autofillBody: Attaching {template}. Fill it with {client}'s stored details and sync any new core facts back to the matter?
workspace.wizardPrefill: [ {section} - pre-filled from template + client record. Edit here. ]
workspace.wizardCompiled: Compiled from template + synced client details.
workspace.documentPlaceholder: The drafted clause body will render here once an AI suggestion is accepted or a template is attached.

firmpulse.title: Firm Pulse
firmpulse.subtitle: {firm} · {region} - the single pane of glass for how healthy the firm is, right now.
firmpulse.throughputSubtitle: Opened vs closed per month · {opened} opened / {closed} closed this month
firmpulse.performanceSubtitle: Evidence for a human decision - the system recommends, the partner decides.
firmpulse.slippingSubtitle: Delay & gap detection across every matter
firmpulse.exceptionSlippingBody: High-severity blockers on active matters. Triage before the clock runs out on a deadline or a client conversation.
firmpulse.exceptionWallBody: Cross-wall access requests awaiting partner sign-off. Default-deny is on; nothing flows until you decide.
firmpulse.exceptionAgedBody: Unbilled work over 45 days erodes realisation and conversation comfort with clients. Bill it or write it off this week.
firmpulse.exceptionConflictsBody: Conflict checks flagged potential or confirmed hits. Clearance - or a wall - needs your sign-off before work continues.
firmpulse.allClearTitle: All clear - nothing needs your attention
firmpulse.allClearBody: No high-severity blockers, no pending wall decisions, no aged WIP, no open conflicts. The firm is running clean.

conflicts.title: Conflicts & ethical walls
conflicts.subtitle: Every new client and adverse party is scanned against your book. Walls isolate sensitive matters by default.
conflicts.listSubtitle: Latest first. Outcome is one of clear, potential, confirmed, or waived.

match.adverse_party_is_existing_client: Adverse party is an existing client
match.adverse_party_matches_existing_matter: Adverse party matches an existing matter

result.clearBody: You can proceed to open the matter.
result.potentialBody: Review the matches. If you can proceed (e.g. with a wall), waive the check.

walls.title: Ethical walls
walls.subtitle: A walled matter is invisible to non-members - they can't list it, open it, search it, or AI-draft from it.
walls.noWalls: No walls in this firm yet.
walls.pendingRequestsSubtitle: Approve only if cross-wall access is genuinely needed. Every decision is logged.

accessLog.title: Access log - who saw what
accessLog.subtitle: Append-only. Every view, download, export, and print is here. Filter by user / matter / action / date.

exportModal.title: Reason for export?
exportModal.body: Firm policy logs every export with the lawyer's stated reason. This row goes to the access log.
exportModal.placeholder: e.g. Counterparty diligence pack - JV closing checklist

upload.title: Drop a document on this slot
upload.body: PDF, DOCX, TXT or MD - drag a file here or click to browse
upload.constraints: Up to 25 MB · text gets extracted for AI context
upload.wallDenied: You aren't on this matter's wall - upload refused
upload.badType: Unsupported file type - use PDF, DOCX, TXT or MD

compare.title: Compare versions
compare.body: Word-level redline between two uploaded versions, with an AI summary of what materially moved.

selection.title: {n} characters selected
selection.pdfHint: Select inside the PDF, then paste the text to use it.
selection.pastePlaceholder: Paste the highlighted clause from the PDF here…

insights.title: Document insights
insights.notReady: AI insights aren't ready yet. Upload a document or wait for parsing to complete.

calendar.title: Compliance calendar
calendar.subtitle: Every regulatory filing your clients owe, grouped by month, with regulator and severity. UAE + KSA covered.

signatures.title: Signatures
signatures.subtitle: Sequential or parallel e-signature workflows with per-party magic links, tamper-evident certificates, and full audit trail.

list.title: Signature workflows
list.subtitle: Each row is a document on its way through signing. Sequential workflows email the next signer automatically.
list.empty: No signature workflows yet - create one from a contract or paste a document directly.

create.title: Create a signature workflow
create.body: Paste the document body in markdown, list the signing parties, choose sequential or parallel. Lexoni emails each party their own magic link.
create.ok: Workflow created - review the parties, then send.
create.errFields: Title, body and at least one party (name + valid email) are required.

send.ok: Sent - sequential workflows will progress one signer at a time.

engagement.title: Engagement letters
engagement.subtitle: Auto-drafted from intake or matter context, sent for digital signature, executed back into the matter.

list.title: Engagement letters
list.subtitle: Status flows: draft → sent → viewed → client-signed → executed.
list.empty: No engagement letters yet - generate one from an intake or directly here.

generate.title: Generate an engagement letter
generate.body: Drafts a partner-grade engagement letter using AI, persists it in draft state, and prepares it for sending.
generate.errFields: Add a client name and a valid email.

billing.title: Billing & invoices
billing.subtitle: Subscription, invoices, payments and usage. Every change writes to audit_log.

wip.title: Unbilled WIP - ready to invoice
wip.subtitle: Confirmed time entries on each matter that haven't been rolled into an invoice yet.
wip.empty: Nothing unbilled - every confirmed hour is on an invoice.
wip.noneToInvoice: No unbilled time on this matter.

invoices.title: Invoices
invoices.subtitle: Issued invoices, outstanding balances and payment history.

pay.title: Record a payment
pay.body: Logs the payment against this invoice. The invoice status updates automatically and the event is audited.
pay.referenceHint: Optional - wire reference, cheque number, transaction id.

usage.title: Usage (last 30 days)
usage.subtitle: Per-event charges - document generation credits, marketplace template purchases, white-label setup.
usage.empty: No usage events in the last 30 days.

members.title: Members & invites
members.subtitle: Who is in your firm, what they can do, and who is supervising whom. Every change writes to audit_log.

list.title: Active members
list.subtitle: Roles, supervision and billing rate. Deactivating a member keeps the audit trail intact.

invites.title: Pending invites
invites.subtitle: Invites expire after 7 days. Cancel any that shouldn't proceed.
invites.modalBody: We'll create a magic-link token for this email. The colleague accepts and the membership is created with the role you set here.
invites.fNamePlaceholder: Optional - they can fill this in on accept
invites.fReportsHint: Only required for lawyer helpers / paralegals.
invites.errorEmail: That doesn't look like a valid email.
invites.errorAlready: This person is already a member of your firm.
invites.errorDuplicate: An invite for this email is already pending.
invites.errorGeneric: Couldn't send the invite. Try again.

gdpr.title: Data & privacy
gdpr.subtitle: Data-subject requests, processing register, consent ledger and retention policy. All times stamped, all reads tenant-scoped.

dsr.title: Data-subject requests
dsr.subtitle: Each request runs on a 30-day clock under GDPR, UAE PDPL and KSA PDPL - whichever applies to the controller tenant.
dsr.empty: No DSRs received. Nothing to action.

ropa.title: Record of processing activities
ropa.subtitle: GDPR Article 30 register. Each activity declares its purpose, lawful basis, data categories, retention.
ropa.body: Reviewed annually by the firm's DPO. Edits create new versions; the prior version is retained for audit.
ropa.empty: No activities registered. Add at least one before the DPO review.

consent.title: Consent ledger
consent.subtitle: Per-subject consent records with proof of capture. Revocations land here too.
consent.body: Every consent capture writes evidence (signature / IP / source URL) for later audit.

policy.title: Retention & residency policy
policy.subtitle: Single page summary partners can paste into a vendor questionnaire.
policy.residencyBody: Data is pinned to your tenant's region. Cross-border movement requires a logged lawful basis.
policy.encryptionBody: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit. Customer-managed keys available on Enterprise.
policy.accessBody: Every Drizzle read filters by session.tenantId via the scoped() helper. Walled matters apply default-deny.
policy.retentionBody: Derived from your RoPA rows. Update RoPA to change a band.

guarantees.title: Compliance guarantees
guarantees.subtitle: Architectural commitments your DPO can verify in code.
guarantees.tenant: Every Drizzle read filters by tenant_id via the scoped() helper.
guarantees.residency: Data is region-pinned to {region}; cross-border requires a logged lawful basis.
guarantees.audit: Every view, download, export, signature and AI suggestion lands in append-only audit_log + access_log.
guarantees.export: Subject access exports are produced from the same data layer the app reads - no shadow copy.

cmdk.title: Command palette
cmdk.empty: No results. Try a matter number, lawyer name, or action.

coaching.tipOn: Junior tips on - hover any underlined term, click (i) for step guidance
coaching.firstHintBody: Underlined terms have a definition on hover. The (i) next to each step explains why it exists, common mistakes, and what to do next. You'll also be asked to walk a quick checklist before any send-for-signature.
coaching.promptCoachHint: Tip: add jurisdiction, language, parties, or a term-sheet constraint to get a usable draft.

promptCoach.title: Tip from the partner desk
promptCoach.vague: Sharpen your prompt. Add: which clause, jurisdiction (e.g. DIFC), parties' obligations, and any term-sheet constraints. The AI grounds better with specifics.
promptCoach.missingJurisdiction: Mention the jurisdiction (DIFC/ADGM/DMCC/MISA) - clauses vary by free zone.
promptCoach.missingLanguage: If you need the Arabic version, ask for «formal legal Arabic, not literal translation».

toast.populated: Populated from {client} · core matter details synced
toast.draftReady: AI drafted into "{title}" - citing firm clause library. Awaiting your review.
toast.draftFailed: Couldn't generate the draft - try again
toast.wallDenied: You aren't on this matter's wall - drafting refused
